relationship_explanation: “Runtime Platform and Operations Scope” defines the runtime-wide scope or governance boundary across Streamlit, AppController, SuperPrompt, configuration, persistence, deployment, and planned interfaces; no one Python file owns that complete normative scope.

“Shared GHOST Core Boundary” governs the relationship among ragstream/app/controller.py, ragstream/preprocessing/preprocessing.py, ragstream/agents/a2_promptshaper.py, SuperPrompt components, and interface adapters; multiple independent files own essential parts, so the UID is system-wide.

“Interface Adapter Boundary” governs the relationship among ragstream/app/controller.py, ragstream/preprocessing/preprocessing.py, ragstream/agents/a2_promptshaper.py, SuperPrompt components, and interface adapters; multiple independent files own essential parts, so the UID is system-wide.

“Shared Controller Invocation” cannot be fully verified until the MCP implementation exists. Current preprocessing and A2 ownership is centralized in ragstream/app/controller.py and subordinate core modules, but there is no second interface implementation to audit for reuse versus duplication.

“Current Streamlit Runtime Boundary” spans ragstream/app/ui_streamlit.py session_state creation, ragstream/app/controller.py construction, SuperPrompt instances, and deployed process behavior; ui_streamlit.py is central but the complete runtime boundary also includes controller/state/deployment concerns, so no one Python file owns the full UID.

“Planned MCP Runtime Boundary” depends on the planned MCP adapter. The repository currently has Streamlit session handling in ragstream/app/ui_streamlit.py and shared stage methods in ragstream/app/controller.py, but no MCP server/controller path exists to satisfy this UID.

“MCP Requirement Ownership Boundary” depends on the planned MCP adapter. The repository currently has Streamlit session handling in ragstream/app/ui_streamlit.py and shared stage methods in ragstream/app/controller.py, but no MCP server/controller path exists to satisfy this UID.
“Future FastAPI Runtime Boundary” is explicitly future. No FastAPI backend, React session API, shared session store, or cross-interface session manager currently implements the required behavior.
“No Interface-Specific Core Duplication” describes planned cross-interface runtime behavior for which no complete current implementation artifact exists.

“Runtime Process Isolation” is an operational service requirement spanning Python entry points, Docker/container configuration, service-manager behavior, logs, and deployment state; no single Python file implements the complete lifecycle scope.
